title: Kinetics of thermal dehydration and decomposition of hydrated chlorides of some 3d transition metals (Mn-Cu series). Part-V. Dehydration and decomposition of CuCl2.2H2O
author: S. B. Kanungo
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Regional Research Laboratory. Bhubaneswar-751 013, India 

  Manuscript received 9 September 2003, revised 30 August 2004, accepted 2 September 2004  

 Unlike dehydration of the dihydrade chlorides of other metal ions of Mn-Cu series, CuCl 2 ,2H 2 O dehydrates in one step without pre-melting. The anhydrous salt decomposes in air three steps yielding CuO as final product. The kinetics of dehydrartion studied by non-isothermal method show that while in static air progress of reaction interface (R3) fits the data best, in flowing nitrogen random nucleation within bulk material (FI) appears to be the best fit model. However, the rate of first step of decomposition follows nucleation and growth (A3) with an  E  value of 106.2 kJ mol -1 .
